ogTitle: Hollands Landing vs Homebush: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hollands Landing and Homebush.
heading: Hollands Landing vs Homebush — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hollands Landing, Victoria and Homebush, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Homebush has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Hollands Landing (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hollands Landing or Homebush safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hollands Landing has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Homebush scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hollands Landing scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Homebush scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hollands Landing or Homebush?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Homebush has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
